title: Mathematics
subtitle: Week 3: [Advanced] Parallel, Intersecting, and Perpendicular Lines

slides.title: [Advanced] Lines in Our Environment
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Let's explore how lines relate to each other in space. Lines can run side-by-side, cross each other, or meet at special angles to form shapes. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Line relationships = shapes and positions. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Can you look around the room and find where two lines meet?

examples.title: Example 1
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Line relationships = shapes and positions.

slides.title: [Advanced] What are Parallel Lines?
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Parallel lines are straight lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart. No matter how far they extend, they will never cross. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Parallel lines = never meet, constant distance. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Can you draw two parallel lines on your paper using a ruler?

examples.title: Example 2
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Parallel lines = never meet, constant distance.

slides.title: [Advanced] Parallel Lines in Daily Life
slides.text: Advanced analysis: We see models of parallel lines everywhere. The rails of a train track, the opposite edges of a ruler, or parallel crop rows in Polomolok farms. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Crop rows = parallel paths in farms. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Why must train tracks be parallel to each other to be safe?

examples.title: Example 3
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Crop rows = parallel paths in farms.

slides.title: [Advanced] Recognizing Parallel Lines
slides.text: Advanced analysis: We can test if two lines are parallel by measuring the perpendicular distance between them at different points. The distance must be exactly the same everywhere. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Distance d1 = d2 at all points. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. If the distance between two paths changes, are they still parallel?

examples.title: Example 4
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Distance d1 = d2 at all points.

slides.title: [Advanced] What are Intersecting Lines?
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Intersecting lines are straight lines that cross or meet each other at a single shared point. We call this crossing point the point of intersection. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Intersecting lines = cross at a single point. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Can you find where two streets cross to make an intersection?

examples.title: Example 5
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Intersecting lines = cross at a single point.

slides.title: [Advanced] Finding Intersecting Lines
slides.text: Advanced analysis: When two lines cross, they form an X-like shape. The point where they meet is a point that lies on both lines at the same time. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Intersection = shared crossing dot. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. How many points do two straight intersecting lines share with each other?

examples.title: Example 6
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Intersection = shared crossing dot.

slides.title: [Advanced] What are Perpendicular Lines?
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines. When they cross, they form four equal right angles that look like square corners. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Perpendicular lines = cross at a right angle. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. What symbol do we use to show that lines are perpendicular?

examples.title: Example 7
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Perpendicular lines = cross at a right angle.

slides.title: [Advanced] Understanding the Right Angle
slides.text: Advanced analysis: A right angle is an angle of exactly ninety degrees. It forms a perfect L-shape, like the corner of your classroom blackboard or book cover. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Right angle = exactly 90 degrees. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. How can you use a piece of paper to check if an angle is right?

examples.title: Example 8
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Right angle = exactly 90 degrees.

slides.title: [Advanced] Perpendicular Lines in Real Life
slides.text: Advanced analysis: We see perpendicular lines in many places. Street crossings in Koronadal City, window frames, or the corner joints of a wooden pineapple crate are perpendicular. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Street corner = 90 degree crossing. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Why are building walls built perpendicular to the flat floor surface?

examples.title: Example 9
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Street corner = 90 degree crossing.

slides.title: [Advanced] Comparing Line Relationships
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Let's compare: parallel lines never meet. Intersecting lines meet at any angle. Perpendicular lines meet at exactly a ninety-degree right angle in a corner. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Parallel vs Intersecting vs Perpendicular. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Which type of lines form the corner of a square tile?

examples.title: Example 10
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Parallel vs Intersecting vs Perpendicular.

slides.title: [Advanced] Drawing Parallel Lines
slides.text: Advanced analysis: To draw parallel lines, we place our ruler flat, draw one line along the top edge, slide the ruler down, and draw the second line. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Step-by-step ruler guide. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. How does a ruler help us keep the distance between lines equal?

examples.title: Example 11
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Step-by-step ruler guide.

slides.title: [Advanced] Drawing Perpendicular Lines
slides.text: Advanced analysis: To draw perpendicular lines, we draw a flat horizontal line first, then use a square template or protractor to draw a vertical line straight up. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Drawing square corner angles. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Why is a square corner template helpful when drawing perpendicular paths?

examples.title: Example 12
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Drawing square corner angles.

slides.title: [Advanced] Drawing Intersecting Lines
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Drawing intersecting lines is simple. We draw two straight lines that cross each other at any angle, and mark the crossing point with a dot. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Creating cross line patterns. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Can you label the intersection point of your lines as Point P?

examples.title: Example 13
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Creating cross line patterns.

slides.title: [Advanced] Drawing Equal Line Segments
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Let's draw two different line segments of equal length. We use a ruler to draw Segment AB as 6 cm, and Segment CD as 6 cm. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Length AB = Length CD = 6 cm. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Does the direction of the segment change its measured length value?

examples.title: Example 14
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Length AB = Length CD = 6 cm.

slides.title: [Advanced] Measuring Equal Segments
slides.text: Advanced analysis: We can verify that two segments are equal by measuring both with a metric ruler. If both measure fifty millimeters, they are equal in length. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: AB = CD = 50 mm. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Why is accurate measurement important when cutting wood to build frames?

examples.title: Example 15
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: AB = CD = 50 mm.

slides.title: [Advanced] Parallel Weaving in Lake Sebu
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Weavers in Lake Sebu use parallel stripes in their patterns. They align colorful threads side-by-side to create symmetric borders on their T'nalak cloth. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Parallel stripes = traditional T'nalak design. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. How do parallel lines make hand-woven patterns look neat and organized?

examples.title: Example 16
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Parallel stripes = traditional T'nalak design.

slides.title: [Advanced] Grid Layouts in Communities
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Town planners in Tupi design community grids using parallel and perpendicular streets. This makes it easy to organize blocks of houses and parks. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Street grids = parallel and perpendicular blocks. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. How does a grid layout help people navigate a town easily?

examples.title: Example 17
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Street grids = parallel and perpendicular blocks.

slides.title: [Advanced] Finding Corners in Shapes
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Rectangles and squares are made of perpendicular lines. Every corner of a rectangle is a right angle where two perpendicular sides meet each other. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Square corners = perpendicular sides of rectangles. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. How many right angles can you find in a standard square?

examples.title: Example 18
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Square corners = perpendicular sides of rectangles.

slides.title: [Advanced] Line Types in Alphabet Letters
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Look at alphabet letters: H has parallel and perpendicular lines. X has intersecting lines. T has perpendicular lines. E has parallel and perpendicular lines. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Letter lines = parallel, intersecting, perpendicular. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Can you find a letter in your name that has parallel lines?

examples.title: Example 19
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Letter lines = parallel, intersecting, perpendicular.

slides.title: [Advanced] Mount Matutum Trail Maps
slides.text: Advanced analysis: On a map of Mount Matutum trails, hiking paths represent lines. Some trails run parallel to each other, while others intersect at camp locations. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Trail crossing = intersecting paths. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. What does an intersection of two trails represent for hikers?

examples.title: Example 20
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Trail crossing = intersecting paths.

slides.title: [Advanced] Building Wooden Crates
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Farmers build wooden crates for pineapples using perpendicular joints. Connecting the wooden boards at ninety-degree right angles makes the crates strong and stable. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Crate joints = perpendicular corner connections. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. What would happen if the crate corners were not perpendicular joints?

examples.title: Example 21
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Crate joints = perpendicular corner connections.

slides.title: [Advanced] Common Error: Non-Parallel Lines
slides.text: Advanced analysis: A common error is drawing two lines that look parallel but are slightly tilted. If extended far enough, these lines will eventually intersect each other. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Non-parallel lines = eventually cross in distance. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. Why must we measure the distance at both ends to confirm parallel lines?

examples.title: Example 22
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Non-parallel lines = eventually cross in distance.

slides.title: [Advanced] Common Error: Tilted Intersections
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Another mistake is drawing intersecting lines that look perpendicular but are slightly tilted. We must verify they form a true ninety-degree angle using a corner. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Tilted crossing = not perpendicular. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. How can you verify if two crossed lines are perpendicular?

examples.title: Example 23
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Tilted crossing = not perpendicular.

slides.title: [Advanced] Line Relationship Exercises
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Let's practice identifying lines. Look at different pairs of lines on your worksheet and classify them as parallel, intersecting, or perpendicular based on their crossings. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Classify line pairs = parallel, perpendicular, intersecting. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. What type of lines do the hands of a clock make at three?

examples.title: Example 24
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Classify line pairs = parallel, perpendicular, intersecting.

slides.title: [Advanced] Summary of Week 3 Lessons
slides.text: Advanced analysis: Let's review: we learned to identify and draw parallel lines, intersecting lines, and perpendicular lines, and to draw equal line segments using a metric ruler. In math, we express this as: Advanced math: Line types = parallel, perpendicular, and intersecting. Let's learn and master this key mathematical concept. Parallel lines are lines on a flat surface that always stay the same distance apart and never cross. Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at a single shared point called the intersection. Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross to form a right angle. A right angle is exactly ninety degrees, which looks like the square corner of a book or sheet of paper. How do these line relationships help us draw shapes accurately?

examples.title: Example 25
examples.content: Review standard details: Advanced math: Line types = parallel, perpendicular, and intersecting.

challenge.q: [Advanced HOTS] Line A is parallel to Line B. Line B is parallel to Line C. What is the relationship between Line A and Line C?
options: They are parallel to each other

pages:
Exercise 1: Identifying Types of Lines

Write Parallel, Intersecting, or Perpendicular for each pair of lines below.

1. Lines that cross and form four square corners:
Perpendicular
2. Lines that run side-by-side and never cross:
Parallel

Exercise 2: Drawing Equal Line Segments

Answer the questions about ruler measurements and line segment lengths.

1. To draw a line segment of exactly 8 cm, where should you place the start point on the ruler?
0 mark

Exercise 3: Geometry in Art

Identify geometric line patterns found in local South Cotabato arts.

1. What type of line patterns are formed by the crossing warp and weft fibers in T'nalak weaving?
Intersecting

performance.title: [Advanced] Street Map Designer with Lines
performance.desc: Advanced Task: On a clean sheet of paper, design a simple road map for a barangay in Polomolok. Draw at least two parallel roads (label them Pineapple Road and Matutum Road). Draw another road that intersects both parallel roads (label it Mountain Road). Finally, draw a road that is perpendicular to Pineapple Road (label it Market Road). Draw two equal-sized houses using your ruler. Take a photo of your labeled map and upload it here.
labels: Correct parallel and perpendicular roads drawing
Correct drawing of two equal-sized houses using a ruler
